User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Prompt Refinery application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

Prompt.title: Prompt
Prompt.description: Represents a prompt created or refined by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Prompt entity.

originalPrompt.description: The original prompt entered by the user.

refinedPrompt.description: The refined prompt generated by the AI Council.

promptType.description: The type of prompt (e.g., Zero-shot, Few-shot).

creationTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the prompt was created.

CouncilRefinement.title: CouncilRefinement
CouncilRefinement.description: Represents a single refinement step by a member of the AI Council.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CouncilRefinement entity.

councilMember.description: The name or identifier of the AI Council member providing the refinement.

thoughtProcess.description: The thought process or reasoning of the council member during the refinement.

refinedText.description: The refined text or suggestion provided by the council member.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the refinement was made.

SavedPrompt.title: SavedPrompt
SavedPrompt.description: Represents a prompt saved by a user for future use.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavedPrompt entity.

saveTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the prompt was saved.

definition.description: Stores user profile information.  Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their own profile data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores prompts created by the user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their own prompts. Includes userId to redundantly ensure no one creates a prompt assigned to a wrong user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: promptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the prompt.

definition.description: Stores AI council refinements for a specific prompt.  Path-based ownership ensures that refinements are only accessible in the context of a user's prompt.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: promptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the prompt.

params.name: refinementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the council refinement.

definition.description: Stores prompts saved by the user for future use. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their saved prompts. Includes userId to redundantly ensure no one saves a prompt assigned to a wrong user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: savedPromptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the saved prompt.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the Prompt Refinery application, focusing on user-owned prompts, AI council refinements, and saved prompts. The structure ensures authorization independence by avoiding hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules. User-owned data is stored under `/users/{userId}` paths. This design facilitates secure `list` operations and maintains data integrity through clear ownership and explicit state modeling. 

**Authorization Independence**: Achieved through the path-based ownership model, ensuring that access control is determined by the user ID in the path, rather than relying on data from parent documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)**: Achieved by segregating user data within their own path-based ownership structure (`/users/{userId}/prompts/{promptId}`).  This allows for secure listing of prompts owned by a specific user without exposing data from other users. Similarly, the `savedPrompts` subcollection is secured under the same path-based ownership.

The `CouncilRefinement` subcollection does not require denormalization since security rules are based on the parent `Prompt`'s `userId`.  Listing refinements is only allowed if the user owns the parent `Prompt` document.
